pull_requests.title: Replace conda+requirements.txt setup with pixi+pyproject.toml
pull_requests.body:
- Replaces conda and requirements.txt setup with pixi and pyproject.toml
- Updates docs build instructions in contributing docs guide
- Updates CI docs build workflow to use pixi

pull_requests.title: Strip inline HTML from prev/next titles
pull_requests.body:
Closes #251 

- overrides `pydata-sphinx-theme`'s [components/prev-next.html](https://pydata-sphinx-theme.readthedocs.io/en/stable/user_guide/layout.html#built-in-components-to-insert-into-sections) template to use Jinja2 [`striptags` filter](https://jinja.palletsprojects.com/en/stable/templates/#jinja-filters.striptags). This change only affects `aeon_acquisition` API reference.

pull_requests.title: Update redirected DataJoint docs URLs to `latest` in querying data example
pull_requests.body: Closes https://github.com/SainsburyWellcomeCentre/aeon_docs/issues/248

pull_requests.title: Update broken and redirected URLs
pull_requests.body:
- `squash_merge_to_prod.yml` has been removed in https://github.com/SainsburyWellcomeCentre/aeon_mecha/pull/550. Removed reference to this workflow file and updated PR workflow description accordingly.
- Updated permanently redirected datajoint docs URLs to point to latest version

resolves #249

pull_requests.title: Improve SVG rendering in light and dark mode
pull_requests.body:
Closes #119 and #208 

- SVGs with line art (transparent/white background, black/coloured lines) rendered with visible white boxes in both light and dark mode.
  - In light mode: mix-blend-mode: multiply removes the baked-in white background so SVGs blend into the surrounding div.
  - In dark mode: filter: invert(1) hue-rotate(180deg) turns black lines white; mix-blend-mode: screen dissolves the inverted black background. A background fix on .sd-card-hover:hover prevents the blend from break

pull_requests.title: Test codecov behaviour
Redirect DataJoint attach downloads to tmp dir in tests and restrict populate calls to experiment being tested
pull_requests.body:
When running integration tests for OnixImuChunk, two related issues cause .joblib files to accumulate in cwd:
- OnixImuChunk.make() calls fetch1("sync_model"), which extracts .joblib files to dj.config["download_path"] (default is cwd) and these are never cleaned up after running the test suite
- All populate() calls are unrestricted. Since MySQL container is session-scoped, EphysSyncModel rows inserted by one test are visible to populate() calls in other tests, causing make() to process rows

pull_requests.title: Mock DJ for all tests marked "unit"
pull_requests.body:
The current setup will only mock DataJoint when the tests are run with `-m unit`. 

Running `uv run pytest tests/dj_pipeline/utils/test_stats_unit.py` for instance will result in the following error:
> FAILED tests/dj_pipeline/utils/test_stats_unit.py::TestColumnStats::test_column_stats[numeric array] - pymysql.err.OperationalError: (2003, "Can't connect to MySQL server on 'localhost' ([WinError 10061] No connection could be made because the targ...

Instead of checking for the `-m` marker,

pull_requests.title: Support PEP 695 `type` statement and python 3.12+ `TypeAliasType`
pull_requests.body:
Closes #690 

This PR
- adds an `if isinstance(annotation, TypeAliasType):` branch in `format_annotation` to handle [typing.TypeAliasType](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.TypeAliasType) introduced in python 3.12
  - the logic is similar to how `TypeAliasForwardRef` is currently handled, extending that same lookup to all intermediate prefix levels, so aliases documented anywhere in the module hierarchy resolve correctly
- adds, for external TypeAliasType (defined in a di

- Also synchronises datajoint_pipeline branch with main

pull_requests.title: Combine lint and test workflows
pull_requests.body:
This PR closes #555 
- combines lint and test in a single lint_and_test.yml
- configures tests to run on
  - ubuntu (all supported python versions, unit + integration tests)
  - macOs and windows (latest supported python version - 3.12, unit tests only as only the ubuntu runner has docker pre-installed)
- removes other unused workflows
- updates docker-aeon-mecha.yml to run on `main` (is this workflow still needed?)
 
Ruff is failing but this can be addressed in a separate PR.

pull_requests.title: Add workflow to squash-merge `datajoint_pipeline` PRs to `main`
pull_requests.body:
This PR replaces the squash-merge-to-prod workflow with one that squash-merges all merged PRs to datajoint_pipeline to `main`. 
The current workflow is to keep the full commit history on `datajoint_pipeline` and a summarised view on `main`, essentially replacing the previous `main`/`prod` with `datajoint_pipeline`/`main`.

pull_requests.title: Add ephys pipeline
pull_requests.body:
Add ephys schemas (v0.2.0).

Adds spike sorting, manual curation, and cross-session unit matching capabilities to aeon mecha.

TODO
- re-release v0.2.0 after this PR is squash-merged to main

pull_requests.title: Preserve images with 0 instances as empty labeled frames in DLC and COCO readers
pull_requests.body:
Closes #417 

This PR
- fixes the COCO reader to create a LabeledFrame for every image in the "images" array, regardless of whether it appears in "annotations"
- fixes the DLC reader to create a LabeledFrame for every CSV row, regardless of whether all coordinates are NaN
- adds tests/data/coco/flat_images/annotations_negative_frame.json, a COCO annotations file with 3 images and 2 annotations (image id 2 has no corresponding annotation to represent a negative frame)
- extends COCO read_la

pull_requests.title: Fetch tags upon checkout in docs build workflow
pull_requests.body: The actions/checkout step does not specify fetch-depth, so it defaults to 1 (a shallow clone with only the latest commit but no tags). In conf.py `git describe --tags --abbrev=0` then fails to find any tag and returns "".

pull_requests.title: Add concurrency settings to docs build workflow
pull_requests.body: This PR configures the docs build workflow such that any in-progress runs are cancelled if a new run is triggered for the same ref, except for tags and main branch pushes.

pull_requests.title: Disable uv caching for publish job in CI workflow
pull_requests.body:
Because the publish step only uses uv for publishing from built files (and not producing any uv-cacheable content, e.g. no dependencies were downloaded by uv), there isn't a cache which setup-uv expects to find at cleanup time.

The alternative would be to keep `enable-cache` as true (default) and set `ignore-nothing-to-cache: true` 

Closes #73
